selected_abstract_preview: Smart Home technology is increasingly seen as a solution for improving household energy efficiency. However, its energy-saving potential depends largely on how consumers use the system. To explore how user perception and intention to use Smart Home can influence energy efficiency, we develop a research model combining the theory of planned behavior (TPB) and the norm activation model (NAM), based on a comprehensive literature review.
